operation: Start a sketch on the XY workplane. Draw a closed 2D profile by first placing a point at (0,0), drawing a 20mm horizontal line to (20,0), creating a 3-point convex arc with midpoint (25,5) that ends at (20,10), drawing a 20mm horizontal line left to (0,10), then drawing a 10mm vertical line down to close the shape. Extrude this profile 8mm along the positive Z axis to form the base solid. Select the topmost Z-facing face, create a workplane aligned to this face, then cut a counterbore hole at the workplane center with a 6mm diameter through hole, 12mm diameter counterbore, and 5mm counterbore depth.
geometry: This is a prismatic solid of uniform thickness, with a cross-section that consists of a rectangular shape where one short edge is replaced by an outward bulging convex semicircular arc. A coaxial counterbore through hole is cut at the center of the part's top flat face, with a wider shallow recess at the top that transitions to a narrower hole running through the full height of the solid.
function: This is a mounting spacer or fastening tab designed for mechanical assembly, with a counterbore hole that allows a fastener head to sit flush with the top surface, and a curved end that mates against matching rounded surfaces for secure, aligned positioning between assembled components.
